creator: thefloridaqueenie
caption:
Looking for things to do around Florida? Comment "Guide" and I'll send you my interactive Florida map and guide with over 1,000 spots to explore! I did the work for you so you can just hit the road without hours of research and stress!

Have you ever been to “Florida’s Little Greece”? 🇬🇷 

This is 📍Tarpon Springs, Florida, the Sponge Capital of the World and the the cutest Greek village outside of Europe! 

Things to do:

🩵Watch the sponge divers sort sponges on the Sponge Docks
💙Explore Main Street
🩵Seafood at Rusty Bellies
💙Greek food at Hellas
🩵Free tour and tasting at Tarpon Springs Distillery 
💙Have beer, nachos and listen to live music at Brighter Days Brewery
🩵Shop for natural sponges and olive oil soap at Lori’s Soaps and Sponges
💙Greek desserts at Hellas or Mykonos Greek Cookery 

#tarponspringsflorida #littlegreece #thingstodoinflorida #floridahiddengems #floridabucketlist

pinned_places: Tarpon Springs, Florida, the Sponge Capital of the World and the the cutest Greek village outside of Europe!

transcription.text: If you want to feel like you're in Greece without ever leaving Florida, then you need to visit this historic Greek town known as Florida's Little Greece. This Greek town is located on Florida's central west coast about 45 minutes from Tampa. Welcome to Tarpon Springs. This town is the sponge capital of the world and has a deeply rooted Greek history. You'll likely see sponge boats hauling in loads of sponges and divers sorting them right on the docks. Let's talk food. Tarpon Springs is also known for its authentic Greek restaurants and Hella's is the most popular. Fresh seafood is also plentiful and Rusty Belly's was incredible. Don't forget the Greek bakeries. There is also the Tarpon Springs distillery where you can do a free tour and tasting. There are also several breweries in town to check out and Better Days Brewery had a good selection of beer, live music, and great nachos. The biggest draw to the area are the sponge docks where you'll find so many cute shops specializing in sponges, soaps, and other trinkets. I love shopping for sponges, olive oil soap, and checking out all the amazing shells. If you're looking for a quaint town with a lot of Greek influence without ever having to leave the country, Tarpon Springs is for you. Be sure to save this post and follow along for more great Florida content from a local.
